name: తెలుగు
register: Telugu, respectful (formal address to and from the Father), contemporary reverent Bible register
quote_convention: Use straight double quotes " " around speech, matching the English source verbatim in count and placement; use single quotes ' ' for a quotation nested inside a quotation.
poetry_convention: Preserve the English poetry line breaks exactly: one Telugu line per English line, same count and order, same indent (the line's numeric indent is copied, only the line text is translated). Do not merge or split lines unless a native reviewer rebreaks them.
reversion_note: The gate in audit_lang.py treats the Telugu renderings of the 'God' and 'the LORD' glossary entries as reversion tokens: they must NOT appear in a verse whose English says 'our Father' / 'the Father' / 'Heavenly Father' / 'Abba'. Those words are only for God-as-God / covenant-name contexts the English itself keeps as 'God' or 'the LORD'.

glossary.note: Divine name. Confirmed in the Telugu preview (Genesis 1:1).
Primary filial divine name. Confirmed in preview. NEVER revert to దేవుడు / యెహోవా.
Aramaic address retained + Telugu gloss. Proposed.
REVERSION TOKEN. Use ONLY where the English retains 'God' as God-as-God; never as a substitute for 'our Father'.
REVERSION TOKEN. Use ONLY where the English retains the covenant name 'the LORD'; never as a substitute for 'our Father'. (Generic title 'Lord' for Jesus = ప్రభువు, distinct.)
Relational, not by-identity. Confirmed in preview (Genesis 1:2).
Christological title. Proposed — native reviewer to confirm vs తండ్రి కుమారుడు.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially. Confirmed in preview (John 3:3,5).
Renders Hebrew hesed (loyal covenant love). Proposed — reviewer to confirm vs స్థిరమైన ప్రేమ.
Renders Hebrew racham / Greek splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy). Proposed.
